perturbation.original_text: 1.24 "Product Specifications" means ENERGOUS' written technical specifications for the Products as referenced in datasheets and related documentation such as errata sheets. All Product Specifications are subject to change with at least one (1) months prior written notice to DIALOG, provided that with respect to any warranty for Products covered by this Agreement, the Product Specification in effect at the time of shipment of the relevant Product will apply for warranty purposes notwithstanding any subsequent change to the Product Specifications as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.24 "Product Specifications" means ENERGOUS' written technical specifications for the Products as referenced in datasheets and related documentation such as errata sheets. All Product Specifications are subject to change without any prior notice to DIALOG.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause about the warranty being tied to the Product Specification in effect *at the time of shipment* and the requirement for *one month's notice* for changes, the modified text introduces uncertainty and potential contradiction with Section 11.2(a). Specifically, 11.2(a) states ENERGOUS warrants that the Products will conform to the Product Specifications. Without the original clause in 1.24, ENERGOUS can change the Product Specifications at any time without notice, potentially invalidating the warranty if a product shipped under older specifications fails to meet new, unannounced specifications. There is an in-text contradiction created between the two clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (including any software updates if any), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for one month's prior written notice for changes to Product Specifications and removes the clause tying the warranty to the Product Specification in effect at the time of shipment. This contradicts Section 11.2(a), which warrants that products conform to Product Specifications. Without the original clause, ENERGOUS can change specifications at any time, potentially voiding the warranty for products already shipped under previous specifications, creating an inconsistency between the definition of "Product Specifications" and the scope of the warranty.

perturbation.original_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (including any software updates if any), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code. The above warranties are valid for a period of [***] from the date of shipment of any Licensed Product to any customer.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.2 Product Warranty. (a) ENERGOUS warrants that (i) when manufactured in accordance with the Approved Production Specifications, and as implemented in a suitable circuit application in accordance with the Product Specifications, the Products and Product Die will conform to the Product Specifications and will be free from defects that could have been avoided in their design; (ii) the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation and the Deposit Materials do not infringe any third party's Intellectual Property Rights; (iii) it did not misappropriate any third party's trade secrets in the development of the Products, any constituent parts or functionality thereof, the Documentation or the Deposit Materials; and (iv) when delivered (including any software updates if any), no Product will contain any viruses, "Trojan horses" or other harmful code.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the warranty period clause from 11.2(a), a contradiction is created with Section 15.4. Section 15.4(a) refers to the survivability of Section 11 (Representations and Warranties; Disclaimers) during the Wind Down Period, with respect to any Products with a secured design win. If there's no specified warranty period in Section 11.2, it's unclear how long the warranties remain in effect during the Wind Down Period, creating an internal contradiction. The removal of the warranty period makes the continuation of the warranty nonsensical, potentially contradicting the intent of 15.4(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.4 Wind Down Period. (a) Notwithstanding any statement in Section 15.3 to the contrary, upon any termination or expiration of this Agreement and until the later to occur of (i) [***] from the Effective Date or (ii) [***] following the effective date of termination or expiration of this Agreement (the "Wind Down Period"), the parties' respective rights and obligations under Sections 2 (License), 3 (Sourcing), 7 (Product Sales), 9 (Royalties and Service Fees), 11 (Representations and Warranties; Disclaimers), 12 (Indemnification), 13 (Limitation of Liability), 14 (Compliance with Laws), 15.2 (Termination), 16 (Escrow) and all Exhibits hereto which are associated with any of the foregoing listed sections will remain in full force and effect as to (A) any Products or repackaged Product Die with respect to which DIALOG or any of its Affiliates has secured a design win at a customer prior to or within one (1) month after the start of the Wind Down Period, or (B) the sale of any MCMs which have been released for production at a foundry, provided, however, that DIALOG's license rights under Section 2.1 (including any sublicenses granted by DIALOG pursuant to Section 2.4) will be non-exclusive during the Wind Down Period.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the warranty period from Section 11.2 directly contradicts Section 15.4, which explicitly relies on Section 11 remaining in effect during the Wind Down Period for Products with secured design wins. Without a defined warranty period in Section 11.2, the continued enforcement of warranties during the Wind Down Period, as mandated by Section 15.4(a), becomes unclear and unenforceable. The omitted clause directly impacts the temporal scope of the warranty, creating a strong contradiction, as the duration is now undefined where it was previously specified, rendering 15.4 meaningless without it.

perturbation.original_text: 9.6 Audit Rights. During the Record Retention Period, ENERGOUS may appoint a mutually agreed independent, internationally recognized third-party certified auditor who will have the right to inspect and copy the Records upon reasonable prior notice, and DIALOG will (and will cause its Affiliates to) allow necessary access including, as applicable, to its premises where such Records are located. ENERGOUS may exercise such right to this independent-third party audit no more than one time per calendar year and each such audit will be conducted during normal business hours. Such audit may also not interfere with DIALOG's or its Affliates' quarterly closing of its books. In the event that such audit reveals an underpayment of Royalties or Service Fees owed by DIALOG, DIALOG will promptly pay ENERGOUS the amount of the underpayment. If such underpayment is in excess of [***] of the Royalties or Service Fee due for the period audited, DIALOG will also reimburse ENERGOUS for its reasonable, out-of-pocket cost of such audit. In the event that such audit reveals an overpayment of Royalties or Service Fees owed by DIALOG, ENERGOUS will promptly pay DIALOG the amount of the overpayment.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.6 Audit Rights. During the Record Retention Period, ENERGOUS may appoint an auditor who will have the right to inspect the Records upon reasonable prior notice, and DIALOG will allow necessary access to its premises where such Records are located. In the event that such audit reveals an underpayment of Royalties or Service Fees owed by DIALOG, DIALOG will promptly pay ENERGOUS the amount of the underpayment.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clauses like 'mutually agreed independent, internationally recognized third-party certified auditor', 'no more than one time per calendar year', 'conducted during normal business hours', 'such audit may also not interfere with DIALOG's or its Affliates' quarterly closing of its books.', and the conditions for reimbursing ENERGOUS audit costs, the modified text in Section 9.6 introduces a contradiction with Section 9.5. Section 9.5 states DIALOG and its Affiliates will keep accurate books and other records, including but not limited to supporting documentation for the Royalties and Service Fees paid hereunder (the "Records"). Without the removed clauses in 9.6, ENERGOUS could potentially appoint a biased auditor, conduct audits excessively, disrupt DIALOG's business operations, and demand reimbursement regardless of the audit outcome, thus creating an internal contradiction in-text. The removal of restrictions on the audit process creates ambiguity about what 'accurate books and other records' means and how those records can be reviewed fairly and reasonably.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.5 Books. With respect to its exercise of the rights and licenses granted in, and payment obligations under, this Agreement, DIALOG and its Affiliates will keep accurate books and other records, including but not limited to supporting documentation for the Royalties and Service Fees paid hereunder (the "Records"). These Records will be maintained for a period of at least three (3) years from the date of the related payment ("Record Retention Period"), notwithstanding any termination of expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9.6 omits critical constraints on ENERGOUS's audit rights, such as the requirement for a 'mutually agreed independent, internationally recognized third-party certified auditor' and limitations on the frequency and timing of audits. This omission contradicts Section 9.5, which mandates that DIALOG keep 'accurate books and other records.' Without the original restrictions, ENERGOUS could potentially conduct unreasonable or biased audits that undermine the accuracy and fairness expected in Section 9.5, thus creating a contradiction.
